content.thinking:
Now I can see the issue clearly. The canary Ingress (`canary-canary`) has `nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/canary-by-header-value: always` instead of `beta`. It should be `beta` to match the `X-Canary: beta` header.

The current state:
- `canary-stable`: Routes `canary.example.com` to `stable-echo` (the stable backend) - this is the main Ingress
- `canary-canary`: Routes `canary.example.com` to `canary-echo` with canary annotations, but `canary-by-header-value` is set to `always` instead of `beta`
The fix is straightforward: I need to change that header value from `always` to `beta` so requests with `X-Canary: beta` actually route to the canary backend. Let me verify the current behavior first with a test.

content.text: The problem is clear. The `canary-canary` Ingress has `canary-by-header-value: always` instead of `beta` — so it triggers for the literal value "always", not "beta". I'll patch that annotation.

input.description: Patch canary Ingress to match X-Canary: beta header value

content.text: Now let me verify both routing paths work correctly:

input.command: kubectl -n demo exec curl-test -- curl -sS -H "Host: canary.example.com" http://ingress-nginx-controller.ingress-nginx.svc/
input.description: Test request without canary header (should return stable)

input.command: kubectl -n demo exec curl-test -- curl -sS -H "Host: canary.example.com" -H "X-Canary: beta" http://ingress-nginx-controller.ingress-nginx.svc/
input.description: Test request with X-Canary: beta header (should return canary)
